notes: Descriptions are written in enumeration/product-listing register to maximize semantic alignment with Titan embeddings.
Cell & Tissue Culture = media, sera, supplements, matrices only. Actual cells belong in Biospecimens.
Furniture & Storage and General Office Supplies were discovered during products clustering; included alongside 11 official categories.
Review boundary between Chemicals & Solvents and Molecular Biology Reagents before finalizing.

anchors.label: Chemicals & Solvents
anchors.description: Laboratory chemicals, solvents, buffers, and reagent-grade materials identified by CAS number, molecular formula, or purity grade. Includes organic compounds, inorganic compounds, acids, bases, salts, ACS-grade chemicals, HPLC-grade solvents, deuterated solvents, reagent solutions, flammable liquids, corrosive materials, oxidizers, powders, crystals, and bulk chemical raw materials defined by molecular weight, purity percentage, and physical state (solid, liquid, gas, powder, crystal, solution, gel).

anchors.label: Molecular Biology Reagents
anchors.description: Reagents for molecular biology experiments including PCR, gene editing, RNA interference, and cloning. Subcategories include: PCR & qPCR Reagents, Cloning & Expression, Gene Editing, RNA Interference, and Sequencing Reagents. Includes PCR master mixes, qPCR master mixes, Taq polymerase, high-fidelity polymerases, reverse transcriptase, PCR primers, probes, siRNA, shRNA, miRNA, gRNA, Cas9 and other Cas nucleases, restriction enzymes, DNA polymerases, RNA polymerases, ligases, kinases, nucleotides, dNTPs, rNTPs, oligonucleotides, synthetic oligonucleotides, DNA ladders, molecular weight markers, plasmid DNA, phage DNA, lambda phage DNA, genomic DNA standards, nucleic acid standards, NGS library preparation kits, sequencing adapters, and other DNA or RNA modifying enzymes. Products are typically identified by target gene, locus, or DNA/RNA region and by target species. Does NOT include antibodies, proteins, or peptides — see Antibodies or Proteins & Peptides.

anchors.label: Lab Supplies & Consumables
anchors.description: Disposable and reusable laboratory consumables and bench supplies used in daily laboratory work. Subcategories include: Bottles & Containers, Caps & Closures, Laboratory Glassware, Tubes & Vials, Pipettes & Tips, Filtration, Microplates & Microscopy Supplies, Tubing & Hoses, Syringes & Needles, Gloves & Protective Wear, Cleaning & Sterilization, Labeling & Tape, Chemical Safety & Storage, General Lab Consumables, and Research Animal Diets & Husbandry Supplies. Includes tubes, microtubes, Eppendorf tubes, centrifuge tubes, conical tubes, plates, microplates, 96-well plates, pipette tips, filters, filter papers, filter membranes, cuvettes, flasks, Erlenmeyer flasks, volumetric flasks, beakers, crystallizing dishes, Pyrex glassware, borosilicate glassware, individual glassware pieces sold as consumables, containers, bottles, vials, syringes, needles, wipes, germicidal wipes, lab wipes, safety supplies, gloves, nitrile gloves, latex gloves, examination gloves, PPE, protective wear, lab coats, safety goggles, face shields, labeling tape, markers, cleaning supplies, sterilization supplies, autoclave bags, microscopy slides, cover slips, tubing, hoses, stopcocks, caps, closures, specimen containers, sample tubes, cryovials, small sample storage boxes, research animal diets, specialty rodent diets, high-fat diets, animal bedding, and general bench consumables. Does NOT include large furniture-scale storage infrastructure, freezers, or refrigerators — see Furniture & Storage. Does NOT include laboratory instruments with electronic components or motorized mechanisms — see Equipment, Instruments & Parts. Does NOT include general administrative office supplies such as paper, pens, or printer supplies — see General Office Supplies.

anchors.label: Antibodies
anchors.description: Primary and secondary antibodies, immunoglobulins, and antibody-based reagents for research, diagnostic, and therapeutic applications. Subcategories include: Primary Antibodies, Secondary Antibodies, Conjugated Antibodies, Isotype Controls, and Antibody Fragments. Primary antibodies bind directly to target antigens (CD4, CD8, p53, Beta-Actin, GAPDH, Ki-67, PD-L1, EGFR, HER2). Secondary antibodies detect primary antibodies (anti-mouse IgG, anti-rabbit IgG conjugated to HRP, FITC, Alexa Fluor). Conjugated antibodies are directly labeled with fluorophores or enzymes (FITC, PE, APC, HRP, biotin). Isotype controls are matched non-specific antibodies (mouse IgG1, rabbit IgG). Antibody Fragments include Fab, F(ab')2, scFv, nanobodies, and single-domain antibodies. All identified by target protein or epitope, host species (mouse, rabbit, goat, rat, donkey, chicken, sheep), clonality (monoclonal, polyclonal, recombinant), reactivity species, and application (flow cytometry, western blot, IHC, immunofluorescence, ELISA, ChIP). Does NOT include non-antibody proteins, cytokines, growth factors, or enzymes — see Proteins & Peptides. Does NOT include nucleic acids or gene editing reagents — see Molecular Biology Reagents. Does NOT include small molecule chemicals — see Chemicals & Solvents.

anchors.label: Proteins & Peptides
anchors.description: Purified recombinant and native proteins, cytokines, growth factors, hormones, and peptides of biological origin. Subcategories include: Recombinant Proteins, Cytokines & Growth Factors, Enzymes, Peptides & Amino Acids, and Albumin & Carrier Proteins. Includes recombinant proteins expressed in E. coli, HEK293, CHO, baculovirus, or yeast systems; cytokines (interleukins, interferons, TNF); growth factors (EGF, FGF, VEGF, TGF-beta, BMP); hormones; non-antibody enzymes; albumin; fibronectin; collagen; structural proteins; carrier proteins; peptide libraries; custom synthesized peptides; amino acid standards; amino acids; amino acid derivatives; polypeptides; and fusion proteins. Products are identified by source organism, expression system, form (lyophilized, liquid, frozen), purity, and biological activity. Does NOT include antibodies or immunoglobulins — see Antibodies. Does NOT include nucleotides, oligonucleotides, or nucleic acid reagents — see Molecular Biology Reagents.

anchors.label: Kits & Assays
anchors.description: Pre-packaged multi-component kits and assay systems for laboratory detection, quantification, and analysis. Subcategories include: ELISA Kits, Cell-Based Assays, Nucleic Acid Extraction Kits, Protein Quantification Kits, and Detection & Imaging Kits. Includes ELISA kits, sandwich ELISA kits, cell viability assay kits (MTT, CellTiter-Glo), apoptosis assay kits (Annexin V, TUNEL, caspase), proliferation assay kits (BrdU, EdU), nucleic acid extraction kits, RNA isolation kits, DNA extraction kits, plasmid miniprep kits, BCA protein assay kits, Bradford assay kits, western blot detection kits, chemiluminescent HRP substrate kits (ECL), immunohistochemistry detection kits, immunofluorescence staining kits, substrates, blocking buffers, diluents, lateral flow assays, and multiplex assay systems.

anchors.label: Cell & Tissue Culture
anchors.description: Media, reagents, and support materials for maintaining and growing cells in vitro. Subcategories include: Culture Media, Sera & Supplements, Matrices & Coatings, Cryopreservation, and Dissociation Reagents. Includes culture media (DMEM, RPMI, MEM, IMDM, F-12, McCoy's), sera (fetal bovine serum FBS, horse serum, human serum), serum-free media, defined media, B-27 supplement, N-2 supplement, GlutaMAX, L-glutamine, growth factors for cell culture, cryopreservation reagents, DMSO (cell culture grade), freezing media, CryoStor, extracellular matrix proteins, Matrigel, collagen coatings, fibronectin coatings, laminin, poly-L-lysine, dissociation reagents, trypsin, TrypLE, Accutase, collagenase, dispase, cell culture plastics, flasks, dishes, and multi-well plates for cell growth. Does NOT include cells themselves — see Biospecimens. Does NOT include general DMSO as a chemical solvent — see Chemicals & Solvents.

anchors.label: Biospecimens
anchors.description: Human and animal biological samples, primary biological materials, and living cells for research. Subcategories include: Human Biospecimens, Animal Biospecimens, Disease State Biospecimens, Marine Biospecimens, Tissue Processing, and Biospecimen Storage & Management. Includes blood, whole blood, plasma, serum, urine, cerebrospinal fluid, synovial fluid, tissue biopsies, tissue sections, FFPE tissue, frozen tissue, DNA extracted from human or animal tissue, RNA samples, PBMCs (peripheral blood mononuclear cells), bone marrow cells, cord blood cells, primary cells, primary hepatocytes, primary neurons, cell lines, immortalized cell lines, iPSCs (induced pluripotent stem cells), stem cells, cancer cell lines, organoids, marine organism tissues, disease-state samples with clinical metadata, tissue fixatives, embedding media, tissue cassettes, biobanking tubes, and other biobanked biological materials from human, mouse, rat, non-human primate, marine species, or other species. Includes normal and disease-state samples. Does NOT include live research animals or animal model systems — see Animal Models.

anchors.label: Animal Models
anchors.description: Live research animal models and model organisms for in vivo studies. Subcategories include: Mouse Models, Rat Models, Zebrafish Models, Non-Human Primate Models, Other Rodent Models, and Invertebrate & Lower Organism Models. Includes mouse models (C57BL/6, BALB/c, NOD/SCID, nude mice, transgenic, knockout, humanized), rat models (Sprague Dawley, Wistar, ZDF), zebrafish lines, cynomolgus and rhesus macaques, guinea pigs, hamsters, gerbils, rabbits, Drosophila melanogaster strains, C. elegans strains, and Xenopus. Identified by species, genetic strain, genetic modification status (wild-type, knockout, transgenic, knock-in, humanized, CRISPR-modified), and vendor. Includes disease models, oncology models, immunodeficient models, reporter lines, and inbred strains. Does NOT include biological samples or tissue harvested from animals — see Biospecimens. Does NOT include research animal diets or husbandry supplies — see Lab Supplies & Consumables.

anchors.label: Equipment, Instruments & Parts
anchors.description: Laboratory instruments, scientific equipment with electronic or motorized components, automated systems, accessories, and replacement parts. Subcategories include: Centrifugation, Liquid Handling, Thermal & Mixing, Imaging & Microscopy, Separation & Purification, Analysis & Detection, and Cell Biology Instruments. Includes centrifuges, microcentrifuges, rotors, electronic pipettors, automated liquid handlers, robotic workstations, PCR thermal cyclers, qPCR instruments, water baths, heat blocks, shakers, vortexers, sonicators, microscopes (light, fluorescence, confocal, electron), gel documentation systems, imagers, HPLC systems, FPLC systems, gel electrophoresis systems, capillary electrophoresis, analytical balances, spectrophotometers, nanodrop instruments, plate readers, flow cytometers, mass spectrometers, NMR instruments, CO2 incubators, biosafety cabinets, cell counters, lyophilizers, autoclave sterilizers, and all instrument accessories, replacement parts, spare parts, and consumable components specific to an instrument. Does NOT include individual glassware, disposable plasticware, or general bench consumables sold independently — see Lab Supplies & Consumables. Does NOT include large laboratory furniture, freezers, refrigerators, or storage cabinets — see Furniture & Storage.

anchors.label: Controls, Calibrators & Standards
anchors.description: Reference materials, quality control samples, calibrators, and certified reference standards for laboratory assay validation and instrument calibration. Subcategories include: QC Controls, Calibrators, Reference Standards, and Proficiency Materials. Includes positive controls, negative controls, assay controls, QC control materials, multi-analyte controls, calibration standards, calibrators, multi-point calibrators, certified reference materials (CRM), proficiency testing samples, EQA materials, standard solutions, molecular weight markers, DNA ladders, protein ladders, NIST-traceable standards, and traceable reference materials. Defined by analyte or parameter being measured, matrix (human blood, synthetic, latex particle, serum), concentration, and regulatory or certification status (FDA cleared, CE marked, RUO, IVD, NIST traceable, CAP, CLIA).

anchors.label: Furniture & Storage
anchors.description: Laboratory furniture, casework, and large-scale storage infrastructure for laboratory environments. Subcategories include: Laboratory Furniture & Benches, Lab Seating, Storage & Organization, and Lab Storage Solutions. Includes laboratory benches, lab tables, workstations, laboratory chairs, lab stools, seating, shelving units, storage shelves, wire shelving, laboratory cabinets, chemical storage cabinets, flammable storage cabinets, acid storage cabinets, under-counter storage, base cabinets, overhead cabinets, refrigerators, laboratory refrigerators, freezers, -20C freezers, ultra-low temperature freezers (-80C freezers), upright freezers, chest freezers, cryogenic storage tanks, liquid nitrogen dewars, large liquid nitrogen storage vessels, and general laboratory furniture and fixed storage infrastructure. Does NOT include small disposable or reusable sample storage items such as individual cryovials, cryo boxes, microcentrifuge tube racks, or tube holders used at the bench — see Lab Supplies & Consumables. Does NOT include laboratory instruments or equipment with electronic components — see Equipment, Instruments & Parts.

anchors.label: General Office Supplies
anchors.description: Non-laboratory administrative and office supplies used in scientific and research workplaces. Includes paper, notebooks, lab notebooks, pens, pencils, markers for writing (not lab labeling), writing instruments, binders, folders, staplers, tape, adhesives, printer supplies, toner cartridges, printer paper, computer accessories, desk accessories, and general administrative and stationery items. Does NOT include laboratory-specific PPE such as nitrile gloves, latex gloves, examination gloves, lab coats, safety goggles, or face shields — see Lab Supplies & Consumables. Does NOT include laboratory-specific cleaning supplies, sterilization wipes, or germicidal wipes — see Lab Supplies & Consumables.
